trigger.reason: Google's AI Search overhaul at I/O 2026 replacing blue links with AI agents is a major product release from a top-tier AI lab with clear potential to trigger sympathy-fade selling across search-adjacent and SaaS sectors.

primary_event.headline: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
primary_event.summary: Google announced a major restructuring of its Search product, replacing traditional blue link results with AI-powered agents. The change has triggered swift user backlash, with DuckDuckGo installs spiking 30% as users seek alternatives. This represents a fundamental shift in how search results are delivered and consumed.

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Google DeepMind's I/O 2026 Search overhaul (AI agents replacing blue links) for sympathy-fade exposure across the candidate basket.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is squarely a search/SEO/content-marketing event, but the basket is dominated by semis (edge AI, photomasks, process control, power) and vertical workflow SaaS (banking, HR, healthcare, mobility). The semis names are all UP today on no candidate-specific news — that's not panic, that's unrelated tape, and they don't fit the screen. The down-movers (NCNO, PAYC, EVH, VRRM) are vertical workflow software with regulatory/contractual moats that have essentially zero overlap with Google's consumer search agent — these are textbook sympathy-fade candidates.

discoveries.name: nCino, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K Risk Factors explicitly call out AI/ML competition and customer adoption risk, but the core business is described as cloud-based banking software embedded with FIs that are 'highly regulated' — Salesforce-platform deployments, multi-year subscriptions (typically 3-5 years, billed annually), with revenue concentration in regulated financial institutions. The AI threat language is generic competitor risk, not search-agent risk.
discoveries.thesis: Google's consumer search agent has no mechanical pathway into nCino's revenue stream — bank loan origination and onboarding sit behind OCC/FDIC oversight, on Salesforce, with multi-year non-cancelable contracts. The sympathy drop on a search-overhaul headline is thematic SaaS-is-doomed reflex, not a re-pricing of any disclosed risk.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce-back over 3-5 days as the search narrative fades; continued customer renewals at standard terms; no commentary from FI customers about AI agent substitution
discoveries.what_kills: A bank or large FI publicly piloting an LLM-agent stack to replace nCino workflows; nCino guiding down on renewal economics; a regulator endorsing AI-agent loan origination
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical-workflow SaaS in regulated industries (banking, healthcare, payroll) sells off on every consumer-AI headline regardless of business-model overlap — the standards/regulatory moat is the actual asset and it's underpriced on these days.
discoveries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: nCino is a SaaS name and the trigger broadly tags SaaS as 'AI-adjacent at risk'; retail sells the basket without distinguishing consumer search exposure from regulated-FI workflow software.
research_pointers: Confirm fiscal 2026 net revenue retention and renewal rates in latest 10-K — has any cohort begun to churn?
Check whether asset-based pricing model rollout is causing real, idiosyncratic price weakness independent of AI panic
Scan FI trade press for any bank-CIO commentary on AI agents replacing core banking software stacks

discoveries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI/ML as both a competitive risk and an internal investment (licensed LLM deployed on internal network for AI-powered tools). Core business is payroll tax compliance across jurisdictions, ACH processing, Vault Visa payroll card — bank-of-record functions that require regulatory licensing and direct financial-institution integrations. No language suggesting search-agent substitution risk.
discoveries.thesis: Paycom is the system of record for employee compensation — payroll tax filing, W-2 generation, ACH funds movement. A Google search-agent product does not interact with payroll tax compliance or fund-flow plumbing. The sympathy drop is pure HCM/SaaS reflex on an unrelated trigger.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable client retention; no guidance cut tied to AI displacement; commentary that Paycom's own AI tools (Beti/IWant) are accretive
discoveries.what_kills: A competitor demonstrating an LLM-agent that handles multi-state payroll tax filing end-to-end; large client losses cited as AI-displacement; embedded-payroll competitors taking share at an accelerating rate (this risk IS in the 10-K and warrants monitoring)
discoveries.what_to_learn: HR/payroll SaaS sells off on every consumer-AI release despite zero overlap with payroll-tax compliance — the consistency of this reflex is itself the edge.
discoveries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PAYC is on the curated AI-adjacent SaaS list and moved meaningfully lower on light volume on a day with no company-specific news, exactly when a major lab AI release headlined the tape.
research_pointers: Monitor embedded-payroll competitive pressure separately — that risk is real and Paycom-specific, distinct from the search-agent trigger
Check whether the move is being driven by index/ETF flows rather than fundamental selling
Verify no insider selling or earnings preannouncement on the date

discoveries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K describes the business as automated photo enforcement (red-light, speed, school bus, bus lane), tolling/violations processing for RACs, and parking solutions — all built on multi-year municipal contracts, physical camera hardware, and court-admissible evidentiary chains. AI is referenced as a regulatory/operational risk Verra itself uses, not as a substitution threat. No mention of search or content-marketing exposure.
discoveries.thesis: Verra's revenue depends on municipal contracts (NYCDOT alone is ~18% of revenue, just renewed for 5 years), physical camera networks, and statutory authorization for automated enforcement. None of these are touched by a Google consumer search redesign. The drop reads as 'IT Services / SaaS-tagged' basket selling.
discoveries.what_confirms: Quick recovery once the search narrative cools; no further negative municipal-contract news; the just-signed NYCDOT renewal performing to plan
discoveries.what_kills: Additional jurisdictions following Ontario in banning automated speed enforcement; loss of one of the three large Commercial Services customers currently in renewal (this risk is now flagged in the 10-Q and is the real Verra-specific story)
discoveries.what_to_learn: Physical-world infrastructure businesses with statutory moats get caught in 'tech selloff' baskets despite zero AI-substitution exposure — the further the business is from a text interface, the more mispriced the sympathy fade.
discoveries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Verra is classified Technology/IT Services and lives in AI-adjacent baskets; on a major AI-lab release day with no Verra-specific news catalyst, it moved meaningfully lower on elevated volume.
research_pointers: Read the 10-Q risk-factor addition carefully — one of the three big Commercial Services customers is in active renewal under a short-term extension; that's the real risk, not search agents
Verify no court ruling or ballot initiative against photo enforcement on the trade date
Compare today's move against peer government-services names to isolate basket-flow vs. name-specific

discoveries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K describes performance-based specialty care management (oncology, cardiology) with payer contracts, risk-sharing arrangements, and significant exposure to Medicaid/Medicare policy. AI is listed as a risk among many. The dominant risks are oncology cost trend, OBBBA Medicaid changes, customer concentration (Molina 25.7%), and risk-corridor exposure — none of which intersect with a Google search release.
discoveries.thesis: Evolent has zero search-overhaul exposure, so the AI-trigger sympathy story is unjustified on the trigger. BUT the stock has been weak for fundamental reasons (oncology cost overruns, Medicare/Medicaid policy headwinds, $3.83 price tape suggests distressed), and today's move could easily be continued idiosyncratic selling unrelated to the trigger. Calling this an 'unjustified panic buy' on a $3.83 stock with real ongoing risk-corridor problems is not the trade this screen is built for.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Distressed names get tagged to every macro/AI headline, but the right call is to separate sympathy-fade from continuing fundamental impairment. Don't buy distressed tape just because today's headline is unrelated.
discoveries.catalyst: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: EVH is on the AI-adjacent value-based-care list, but today's move likely reflects ongoing oncology/Medicaid concerns more than search-agent panic.
research_pointers: Distinguish ongoing oncology cost-trend impairment from sympathy fade — the former is real and persistent
Check Q1 2026 commentary on Performance Suite risk-corridor performance
Don't trade this on a sympathy thesis until the fundamental trajectory clarifies

skipped.reason: Up 6.88% — not a panic-fade candidate; sympathy direction is wrong for this screen.
Up 5.79% — moved with semis higher, not a sympathy-fade victim.
Up 4.55% — semi-cap rally, not a panic sell-off.
Up 4.55% — positive move; AI-buildout beneficiary trading higher, not a fade candidate.
Up 4.50% — positive move; not a sympathy-fade target on a down-trigger day.
Up 3.39% — moved higher; doesn't qualify for the negative-move BUY trigger.
